Zone 1\n    [(60,  200), (50, 120), (100, 330), (_LY2*0.5, _LY2*2)],  # Zone 2\n    [(60,  200), (60, 140), (100, 320), (_LY3*0.5, _LY3*2)],  # Zone 3\n]","newString":"TABLE1_PARAMS = [\n    # Zone 1: full circle (no truncation); x_p ≈ 125 nm (gap~20 nm), Λ_y ≈ 350 nm\n    #   Table 1 design: w_b=110, d_p=100 — gives η_T~87%, η_R~6% at XFOV=-10°\n    [110.0, 50.0,  125.0, 350.0],\n    # Zone 2: Table 1 design: w_b=110, d_p=170\n    #   Best x_p/Ly from grid scan targeting η_T~54%, η_R~46%\n    #   xp=160, Ly=225 → η_T=48.6%, η_R=34.7% at XFOV=-10°  (R[-1,0]=14%)\n    [110.0, 85.0,  160.0, 225.0],\n    # Zone 3: Table 1 design: w_b=100, d_p=196\n    #   Best x_p/Ly from grid scan targeting η_T~27%, η_R~73%\n    #   xp=300, Ly=200 → η_T=25.7%, η_R=55.9% at XFOV=-10°  (R[-1,0]=12.3%)\n    [100.0, 98.0,  300.0, 200.0],\n]\n\n# Optimisation bounds: [w_beam, r_pillar, x_pillar, period_y]\nOPT_BOUNDS = [\n    [(60,  200), (20, 120), ( 60, 380), (150, 600)],   # Zone 1\n    [(60,  200), (50, 130), ( 80, 380), (100, 400)],   # Zone 2\n    [(60,  200), (60, 150), (150, 400), (100, 400)],   # Zone 3\n]","originalFile":"\"\"\"\nTiO2/N-BK7 three-zone metasurface in-coupler RCWA simulation.\n\nReproduces the design from:\n  \"Design and experimental validation of a high-efficiency multi-zone\n   metasurface waveguide in-coupler\", Optical Materials Express, 2025.\n\nKey design choices (Table 1 / Fig 3):\n  - λ = 532 nm, grating period = 453 nm, TiO2 height = 250 nm (shared)\n  - Three zones: widths 1.06 / 1.01 / 0.93 mm\n  - Per-zone targets (Fig 2e): η_T = 96/54/27%, η_R = 4/46/73%\n  - Simulation: TE-polarised source from N-BK7 glass, angle range 41–63°\n    (equivalent to XFOV −10° to +10° by light-reversibility)\n\"\"\"\n\nimport warnings\nwarnings.filterwarnings(\"ignore\")\n\nimport numpy as np\nimport matplotlib\nmatplotlib.use(\"Agg\")\nimport matplotlib.pyplot as plt\nfrom scipy.optimize import minimize, differential_evolution\nimport json\nimport os\n\nimport grcwa\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Physical constants\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nLAMBDA_NM  = 532.0     # wavelength (nm)\nPERIOD_X   = 453.0     # grating period in x (nm)\nHEIGHT_NM  = 250.0     # TiO2 nano-structure height, fixed for all zones (nm)\nT_WG_MM    = 0.5       # waveguide thickness (mm)\nL_INCOUPLER_MM = 3.0   # total in-coupler length (mm)\n# Zone widths from Fig 2(e)\nZONE_WIDTHS_MM = [1.06, 1.01, 0.93]\n# Accumulated zone boundaries [0, 1.06, 2.07, 3.00] mm\nZONE_BOUNDS_MM = [0.0] + list(np.cumsum(ZONE_WIDTHS_MM))\n\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Refractive indices\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef _n_nbk7(lam_um: float) -> float:\n    \"\"\"N-BK7 Sellmeier equation, λ in µm.\"\"\"\n    l2 = lam_um ** 2\n    return float(np.sqrt(\n        1\n        + 1.03961212 * l2 / (l2 - 0.00600069867)\n        + 0.231792344 * l2 / (l2 - 0.0200179144)\n        + 1.01046945  * l2 / (l2 - 103.560653)\n    ))\n\n\nN_BK7  = _n_nbk7(LAMBDA_NM / 1000.0)   # ≈ 1.5195\nN_TIO2 = complex(2.370, 0.001)         # ALD TiO2 at 532 nm (small imaginary for material loss)\nN_AIR  = 1.0\n\n# Ideal per-zone diffraction targets from Fig 2(e) (sum = 100%)\nIDEAL_TARGETS = [\n    (0.96, 0.04),   # Zone 1: η_T, η_R\n    (0.54, 0.46),   # Zone 2\n    (0.27, 0.73),   # Zone 3\n]\n\n# Adjusted realistic targets (sum < 100% due to absorption and other orders)\n# Derived from Fig 3(d-f) simulation curves\nREALISTIC_TARGETS = [\n    (0.75, 0.08),   # Zone 1\n    (0.52, 0.35),   # Zone 2\n    (0.26, 0.60),   # Zone 3\n]\n\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Angle conversions  (grating equation, m = +1 into glass)\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef xfov_to_glass(theta_air_deg: float) -> float:\n    \"\"\"XFOV (air) → equivalent glass angle via +1-order grating equation.\"\"\"\n    s = (np.sin(np.radians(theta_air_deg)) + LAMBDA_NM / PERIOD_X) / N_BK7\n    return float(np.degrees(np.arcsin(np.clip(s, -1, 1))))\n\n\ndef glass_to_xfov(theta_glass_deg: float) -> float:\n    \"\"\"Glass angle → XFOV (air) via −1-order grating equation.\"\"\"\n    s = N_BK7 * np.sin(np.radians(theta_glass_deg)) - LAMBDA_NM / PERIOD_X\n    return float(np.degrees(np.arcsin(np.clip(s, -1, 1))))\n\n\n# XFOV range and corresponding glass angles\nXFOV_DEG    = np.linspace(-10.0, 10.0, 21)\nTHETA_GLASS = np.array([xfov_to_glass(t) for t in XFOV_DEG])\n\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Geometry helpers\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef _make_epsilon_grid(\n    w_beam_nm:    float,   # beam (bar) width in x\n    r_pillar_nm:  float,   # pillar radius\n    x_pillar_nm:  float,   # pillar centre x  (can be > PERIOD_X/2, handled by PBC)\n    period_y_nm:  float,   # y unit-cell size\n    Nx: int = 200,\n    Ny: int = 50,\n) -> np.ndarray:\n    \"\"\"\n    Return flattened Nx*Ny epsilon array for one metasurface unit cell.\n\n    x ∈ [0, PERIOD_X],  y ∈ [0, period_y].\n    Nano-beam  : rectangle of width w_beam centred at x=0 (periodic boundary\n                 ensures wrapping: occupies x ∈ [0, w/2] ∪ [Px−w/2, Px]).\n    Nano-pillar: circle of radius r_p centred at (x_p, period_y/2).\n                 If x_p + r_p > PERIOD_X the circle wraps around x; grcwa\n                 handles this naturally via Fourier periodicity once the grid\n                 is filled with wrapped coordinates.\n    \"\"\"\n    eps_tio2 = N_TIO2 ** 2\n    eps_air  = N_AIR  ** 2\n\n    x = np.linspace(0, PERIOD_X,  Nx, endpoint=False)\n    y = np.linspace(0, period_y_nm, Ny, endpoint=False)\n    XX, YY = np.meshgrid(x, y, indexing=\"ij\")   # shape (Nx, Ny)\n\n    grid = np.full((Nx, Ny), eps_air, dtype=complex)\n\n    # ── nano-beam: centred at x = 0, full y-extent ──────────────────────────\n    half = w_beam_nm / 2.0\n    beam_mask = (XX <= half) | (XX >= PERIOD_X - half)\n    grid[beam_mask] = eps_tio2\n\n    # ── nano-pillar: centred at (x_p, period_y/2), periodic in x ─────────\n    cy = period_y_nm / 2.0\n    # Compute wrapped x-distance to handle circular periodicity\n    dx = XX - x_pillar_nm\n    dx_wrap = dx - PERIOD_X * np.round(dx / PERIOD_X)   # nearest-image in x\n    dy = YY - cy\n    # Also wrap in y (though usually period_y > 2*r_p for zone 1;\n    # zones 2/3 may have pillar at x-boundary only)\n    dy_wrap = dy - period_y_nm * np.round(dy / period_y_nm)\n    dist = np.sqrt(dx_wrap ** 2 + dy_wrap ** 2)\n    grid[dist < r_pillar_nm] = eps_tio2\n\n    return grid.flatten()\n\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Core grcwa RCWA simulation\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef run_rcwa(\n    w_beam_nm:   float,\n    r_pillar_nm: float,\n    x_pillar_nm: float,\n    period_y_nm: float,\n    theta_glass_deg: float,\n    nG: int = 51,\n    Nx: int = 200,\n    Ny: int = 50,\n) -> tuple:\n    \"\"\"\n    Single-angle grcwa RCWA call.\n\n    Source: TE plane wave from glass substrate at polar angle theta_glass_deg.\n    Stack (front → back in grcwa): glass | metasurface | air.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    T_m1 : float  – η_{T,−1}(glass) = η_{T,+1}(air), first-order diffraction\n    R_0  : float  – η_{R,0}(glass), zeroth-order reflection\n    \"\"\"\n    freq = 1.0 / LAMBDA_NM          # in 1/nm (lengths in nm throughout)\n    theta_rad = np.radians(theta_glass_deg)\n\n    L1 = [PERIOD_X,    0.0        ]\n    L2 = [0.0,         period_y_nm]\n\n    obj = grcwa.obj(nG, L1, L2, freq,\n                    theta=theta_rad, phi=0.0, verbose=0)\n\n    # Layers: glass (incident) → patterned metasurface → air (exit)\n    obj.Add_LayerUniform(0.0,       N_BK7  ** 2)          # substrate (semi-∞)\n    obj.Add_LayerGrid   (HEIGHT_NM, Nx, Ny)               # metasurface\n    obj.Add_LayerUniform(0.0,       N_AIR  ** 2 + 0j)     # air (semi-∞)\n\n    obj.Init_Setup(Gmethod=1)    # rectangular G-vector truncation\n\n    eps_flat = _make_epsilon_grid(w_beam_nm, r_pillar_nm,\n                                  x_pillar_nm, period_y_nm, Nx, Ny)\n    obj.GridLayer_geteps(eps_flat)\n\n    # TE excitation from glass (s-amplitude = 1, p = 0)\n    obj.MakeExcitationPlanewave(p_amp=0, p_phase=0,\n                                s_amp=1, s_phase=0,\n                                order=0, direction=\"forward\")\n\n    R_arr, T_arr = obj.RT_Solve(normalize=1, byorder=1)\n\n    G = obj.G  # shape (nG_actual, 2)\n\n    # η_{T,−1,0}: (m,n) = (−1, 0) order in air exit layer\n    idx_m10 = np.where((G[:, 0] == -1) & (G[:, 1] == 0))[0]\n    T_m1 = float(T_arr[idx_m10[0]]) if len(idx_m10) else 0.0\n\n    # η_{R,0,0}: (0,0) order in glass input layer\n    idx_00 = np.where((G[:, 0] == 0) & (G[:, 1] == 0))[0]\n    R_0 = float(R_arr[idx_00[0]]) if len(idx_00) else 0.0\n\n    # Clamp small numerical negatives\n    T_m1 = max(T_m1, 0.0)\n    R_0  = max(R_0,  0.0)\n    return T_m1, R_0\n\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Angle sweep for one zone\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef angle_sweep(\n    params: tuple,\n    theta_glass_arr: np.ndarray = THETA_GLASS,\n    **rcwa_kw,\n) -> tuple:\n    \"\"\"\n    Run RCWA at each angle in theta_glass_arr.\n    params = (w_beam_nm, r_pillar_nm, x_pillar_nm, period_y_nm).\n    Returns (T_arr, R_arr) each shape (n_angles,).\n    \"\"\"\n    w, r, xp, ly = params\n    T_list, R_list = [], []\n    for tg in theta_glass_arr:\n        T, R = run_rcwa(w, r, xp, ly, tg, **rcwa_kw)\n        T_list.append(T)\n        R_list.append(R)\n    return np.array(T_list), np.array(R_list)\n\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Optimisation merit function\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef _merit(x, target_T, target_R, theta_glass_arr, zone_id, **rcwa_kw):\n    \"\"\"\n    MSE between simulated average efficiencies and targets.\n    x = [w_beam, r_pillar, x_pillar, period_y]  (all in nm, un-bounded).\n    \"\"\"\n    w, r, xp, ly = x\n    # Hard constraints\n    if w < 20 or w > 300 or r < 10 or r > 250 or xp < 10 or ly < 50:\n        return 1e6\n    if xp > PERIOD_X + r:   # pillar entirely outside cell even with wrap\n        return 1e6\n\n    T_arr, R_arr = angle_sweep((w, r, xp, ly), theta_glass_arr, **rcwa_kw)\n\n    # Zone 1: balance across full XFOV (weight both ends equally)\n    # Zones 2, 3: optimised primarily for left end of XFOV (−10°)\n    if zone_id == 0:\n        # Average over full XFOV with extra weight on edges\n        weights = np.ones(len(T_arr))\n        weights[0]  = 3.0   # −10° end\n        weights[-1] = 2.0   # +10° end\n        weights /= weights.sum()\n    else:\n        # Weight strongly toward −10° (left end, small glass angle)\n        weights = np.ones(len(T_arr))\n        weights[0] = 5.0\n        weights /= weights.sum()\n\n    T_mean = float(np.dot(weights, T_arr))\n    R_mean = float(np.dot(weights, R_arr))\n\n    loss = (T_mean - target_T) ** 2 + (R_mean - target_R) ** 2\n    return loss\n\n\ndef optimize_zone(\n    zone_id:  int,\n    x0:       list,\n    target_T: float,\n    target_R: float,\n    bounds:   list = None,\n    method:   str  = \"Nelder-Mead\",\n    maxiter:  int  = 300,\n    **rcwa_kw,\n) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"\n    Optimise geometry for a single zone.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    zone_id  : 0, 1, or 2\n    x0       : [w_beam, r_pillar, x_pillar, period_y] initial guess (nm)\n    target_T : target first-order diffraction efficiency (0–1)\n    target_R : target zeroth-order reflection efficiency (0–1)\n    bounds   : list of (lo, hi) for each variable\n    method   : scipy.optimize method\n    \"\"\"\n    theta_arr = THETA_GLASS\n\n    def merit(x):\n        return _merit(x, target_T, target_R, theta_arr, zone_id, **rcwa_kw)\n\n    options = {\"maxiter\": maxiter, \"xatol\": 1.0, \"fatol\": 1e-4}\n\n    if method == \"differential_evolution\":\n        if bounds is None:\n            raise ValueError(\"bounds required for differential_evolution\")\n        result = differential_evolution(merit, bounds, maxiter=maxiter,\n                                        tol=1e-4, seed=42, workers=1)\n    else:\n        result = minimize(merit, x0, method=method, options=options)\n\n    best = result.x\n    T_arr, R_arr = angle_sweep(tuple(best), theta_arr, **rcwa_kw)\n    return {\n        \"params\":  best.tolist(),\n        \"T_array\": T_arr.tolist(),\n        \"R_array\": R_arr.tolist(),\n        \"loss\":    float(result.fun),\n        \"success\": result.success,\n    }\n\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# MFE  — 1D ray-tracing model\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef compute_coupling_efficiency(\n    T_zones:  list,    # [T_arr_zone0, T_arr_zone1, T_arr_zone2]  shape (n_ang,)\n    R_zones:  list,    # [R_arr_zone0, …]\n    xfov_deg: np.ndarray = XFOV_DEG,\n    n_x:      int = 2000,\n) -> np.ndarray:\n    \"\"\"\n    Compute coupling efficiency η_c(θ_i) using a 1-D ray-tracing model.\n\n    For a ray entering at position x_0 in the in-coupler [0, L_total]:\n      • Primary coupling: η_T(zone at x_0)  — fraction entering waveguide.\n      • The trapped beam travels at angle θ_glass; TIR pitch = 2t·tan(θ_glass).\n      • Each time it hits the in-coupler from below: it retains η_R(zone at x_n)\n        of its power (0th-order reflection); the rest diffracts back out (loss).\n    η_c(x_0) = η_T(x_0) · ∏_{n=1}^{N} η_R(x_n)   where x_n = x_0 + n·pitch,\n               N = max n such that x_n < L_total.\n    η_c_total(θ_i) = mean over x_0 ∈ [0, L_total].\n\n    Returns array of coupling efficiencies, shape (n_angles,).\n    \"\"\"\n    L      = ZONE_BOUNDS_MM[-1]                # 3.0 mm\n    bounds = ZONE_BOUNDS_MM                    # [0, 1.06, 2.07, 3.0]\n    x0     = np.linspace(0, L, n_x, endpoint=False)\n    n_zones = len(ZONE_WIDTHS_MM)\n\n    coupling = []\n    for ai, theta_air in enumerate(xfov_deg):\n        # Glass angle and TIR pitch (in mm)\n        tg_rad = np.radians(xfov_to_glass(theta_air))\n        pitch  = 2.0 * T_WG_MM * np.tan(tg_rad)   # mm\n\n        # Zone index for each starting position\n        zone_of = np.searchsorted(bounds[1:], x0)\n        zone_of = np.clip(zone_of, 0, n_zones - 1)\n\n        # Primary coupling: η_T of the zone at x0\n        eta_c = np.array([T_zones[z][ai] for z in zone_of], dtype=float)\n\n        # Secondary interactions: multiply by η_R for each bounce inside coupler\n        # Vectorised over bounces; max bounces = ceil(L/pitch)\n        if pitch > 0:\n            max_bounces = int(np.ceil(L / pitch)) + 1\n        else:\n            max_bounces = 0\n\n        for n in range(1, max_bounces + 1):\n            xn = x0 + n * pitch\n            inside = xn < L\n            if not np.any(inside):\n                break\n            zn = np.searchsorted(bounds[1:], xn[inside])\n            zn = np.clip(zn, 0, n_zones - 1)\n            r_vals = np.array([R_zones[z][ai] for z in zn], dtype=float)\n            eta_c[inside] *= r_vals\n\n        coupling.append(float(np.mean(eta_c)))\n\n    return np.array(coupling)\n\n\ndef compute_mfe(T_zones, R_zones, xfov_deg=XFOV_DEG):\n    \"\"\"Return (mfe, coupling_array).\"\"\"\n    coupling = compute_coupling_efficiency(T_zones, R_zones, xfov_deg)\n    mfe = float(np.min(coupling))\n    return mfe, coupling\n\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Plotting\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef plot_efficiency_curves(\n    T_zones:   list,\n    R_zones:   list,\n    xfov_deg:  np.ndarray = XFOV_DEG,\n    params:    list = None,\n    out_path:  str = \"fig3_efficiency_curves.png\",\n):\n    \"\"\"Reproduce Fig 3(d-f): efficiency vs incident angle for all 3 zones.\"\"\"\n    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(14, 4), sharey=True)\n\n    sec_ax_labels = [f\"{xfov_to_glass(t):.1f}\" for t in xfov_deg[::5]]\n    sec_ax_ticks  = xfov_deg[::5]\n\n    for k, ax in enumerate(axes):\n        T = np.array(T_zones[k]) * 100\n        R = np.array(R_zones[k]) * 100\n        S = T + R\n\n        ax.plot(xfov_deg, T, \"r-o\",  ms=4, lw=1.5, label=r\"$\\eta_{T,+1}$ (sim)\")\n        ax.plot(xfov_deg, R, \"b-s\",  ms=4, lw=1.5, label=r\"$\\eta_{R,0}$ (sim)\")\n        ax.plot(xfov_deg, S, \"k--\",  ms=3, lw=1.0, label=\"Sum\")\n\n        if params is not None:\n            w, r, xp, ly = params[k]\n            ax.set_title(\n                f\"Zone {k+1}\\n\"\n                f\"w={w:.0f} nm, d_p={2*r:.0f} nm\\n\"\n                f\"x_p={xp:.0f} nm, Λ_y={ly:.0f} nm\",\n                fontsize=8,\n            )\n        else:\n            ax.set_title(f\"Zone {k+1}\", fontsize=10)\n\n        ax.set_xlabel(\"Incident angle (deg)\", fontsize=9)\n        ax.set_xlim(-10, 10)\n        ax.set_ylim(0, 105)\n        ax.axvline(0, color=\"gray\", lw=0.5, ls=\":\")\n        ax.legend(fontsize=7, loc=\"upper right\")\n        ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)\n\n        # Secondary x-axis (glass angle)\n        ax2 = ax.twiny()\n        ax2.set_xlim(ax.get_xlim())\n        ax2.set_xticks(sec_ax_ticks)\n        ax2.set_xticklabels(sec_ax_labels, fontsize=6, rotation=30)\n        ax2.set_xlabel(\"Glass angle (deg)\", fontsize=7)\n\n    axes[0].set_ylabel(\"Efficiency (%)\", fontsize=9)\n    fig.suptitle(\n        f\"TiO₂/N-BK7 Metasurface In-Coupler — Efficiency Curves\\n\"\n        f\"λ=532 nm, period={PERIOD_X:.0f} nm, height={HEIGHT_NM:.0f} nm\",\n        fontsize=10,\n    )\n    fig.tight_layout()\n    fig.savefig(out_path, dpi=150, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\n    plt.close(fig)\n    print(f\"Saved: {out_path}\")\n\n\ndef plot_mfe_curve(\n    coupling:  np.ndarray,\n    xfov_deg:  np.ndarray = XFOV_DEG,\n    mfe:       float = None,\n    out_path:  str = \"fig4_coupling_efficiency.png\",\n):\n    \"\"\"Reproduce Fig 4(b) cross-section: coupling efficiency vs XFOV.\"\"\"\n    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 4))\n    ax.plot(xfov_deg, coupling * 100, \"b-o\", ms=5, lw=2, label=\"Simulation\")\n    if mfe is not None:\n        ax.axhline(mfe * 100, color=\"r\", ls=\"--\", lw=1.5,\n                   label=f\"MFE = {mfe*100:.1f}%\")\n    ax.axhline(25.3, color=\"g\", ls=\":\", lw=1.5, label=\"Paper MFE = 25.3%\")\n    ax.set_xlabel(\"X-FOV (degrees)\", fontsize=11)\n    ax.set_ylabel(\"Coupling efficiency (%)\", fontsize=11)\n    ax.set_title(\"Three-Zone Metasurface In-Coupler — Coupling Efficiency\\n\"\n                 \"(XFOV cross-section)\", fontsize=10)\n    ax.set_xlim(-10, 10)\n    ax.set_ylim(0, 80)\n    ax.legend(fontsize=9)\n    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)\n    fig.tight_layout()\n    fig.savefig(out_path, dpi=150, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\n    plt.close(fig)\n    print(f\"Saved: {out_path}\")\n\n\ndef plot_zone_geometry(params_list, out_path=\"zone_geometry.png\"):\n    \"\"\"\n    Visualise the top-down epsilon map for each zone unit cell.\n    \"\"\"\n    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(12, 4))\n    for k, ax in enumerate(axes):\n        w, r, xp, ly = params_list[k]\n        Nx, Ny = 300, 80\n        eps = _make_epsilon_grid(w, r, xp, ly, Nx, Ny)\n        eps_2d = in red)\", fontsize=10)\n    fig.tight_layout()\n    fig.savefig(out_path, dpi=150, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\n    plt.close(fig)\n    print(f\"Saved: {out_path}\")\n\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Default starting geometry (from Table 1, with estimated x_p / period_y)\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n#\n# Geometry interpretation (confirmed by SEM in Fig 5 and paper text):\n#   • The grating period is in x (Λ_x = 453 nm).\n#   • Each unit cell has a nano-beam (running the full y-extent) and a\n#     nano-pillar (circular cylinder).\n#   • For zones 2 & 3 the pillar diameter d_p EXCEEDS Λ_y, so the pillar is\n#     truncated at the y-boundaries, appearing as a \"partial circle\" in SEM.\n#   • \"Pillar width\" w_p (Table 1) = chord length in x at the y=0 boundary:\n#       w_p = 2·√(r_p² − (Λ_y/2)²)  →  Λ_y = 2·√(r_p² − (w_p/2)²)\n#   • x_p (pillar centre x) is between beam right edge and PERIOD_X − beam_half,\n#     estimated from figures (~200 nm from left edge for zones 2/3).\n\ndef _lambda_y_from_truncation(r_nm: float, w_p_nm: float) -> float:\n    \"\"\"Λ_y given pillar radius r_p and chord width w_p at y-boundary.\"\"\"\n    val = r_nm**2 - (w_p_nm / 2.0)**2\n    return float(2.0 * np.sqrt(max(val, 0.0)))\n\ndef _x_pillar_from_truncation(r_nm, w_p_nm):\n    \"\"\"Legacy: x_p if truncation were in x direction (not used for Table-1 params).\"\"\"\n    delta = float(np.sqrt(max(r_nm**2 - (w_p_nm/2)**2, 0)))\n    return PERIOD_X - delta\n\n\n# Λ_y for zones 2 & 3 from y-truncation condition (Table 1 designed values)\n_LY2 = _lambda_y_from_truncation(85.0,  156.5)   # zone 2: ~66.4 nm\n_LY3 = _lambda_y_from_truncation(98.0,  160.5)   # zone 3: ~112.4 nm\n\nTABLE1_PARAMS = [\n    # Zone 1: full circle (no truncation); x_p ≈ 125 nm (gap~20 nm), Λ_y ≈ 350 nm\n    [110.0, 50.0,  125.0, 350.0],\n    # Zone 2: d_p=170, w_p=156.5 → Λ_y=66.4 nm; x_p=200 nm (between beam & right edge)\n    [110.0, 85.0,  200.0, _LY2],\n    # Zone 3: d_p=196, w_p=160.5 → Specific requirements:\n   - Optimize nano-beam + nano-pillar geometry per zone to hit the per-zone first-order diffraction (η_T) and zeroth-order reflection (η_R) targets from Fig 2(e)\n   - Verify MFE ≥ 25% across ±10° FOV (paper achieves 25.3%)\n   - Produce efficiency-vs-angle curves for each zone matching Fig 3(d-f) shape\n\n2. Key Technical Concepts:\n   - **RCWA (Rigorous Coupled Wave Analysis)**: used via `grcwa` Python package (not the original S4 from Stanford — that was unavailable; \"S4\" pip package is an S3 file sync tool)\n   - **Three-zone metasurface in-coupler**: each zone has nano-beam (bar) + nano-pillar (TiO2 on N-BK7), period=453 nm, height=250 nm fixed\n   - **Equivalent interaction principle**: light reversibility allows computing η_{T,+1}(air) = T[-1,0](glass) and η_{R,0}(glass) = R[0,0](glass) in a single simulation with source inside the glass at θ_glass\n   - **Grating equation**: n_d sin(θ_d) = n_i sin(θ_i) + mλ/d → θ_glass(XFOV=-10°)=41.19°, θ_glass(XFOV=0°)=50.61°, θ_glass(XFOV=+10°)=62.52°\n   - **1D ray-tracing MFE model**: η_c(x₀) = η_T(zone at x₀) × Π η_R(zone at x₀+n×pitch), pitch=2×t_wg×tan(θ_glass), t_wg=0.5 mm, L_total=3 mm\n   - **N-BK7 Sellmeier equation**: N_BK7=1.5195 at 532 nm; critical angle=41.16° (XFOV=-10° is just barely above critical)\n   - **Λ_y derivation**: For zones 2/3 where pillar exceeds cell boundary: w_p = 2√(r_p² - (Λ_y/2)²) → Λ_y = 2√(r_p² - (w_p/2)²)\n   - **grcwa per-order output**: `RT_Solve(normalize=1, byorder=1)` returns arrays indexed by G-vector; key orders T[G=(-1,0)] and R[G=(0,0)]\n\n3. Files and Code Sections:\n   - **`/project/rcwa_metasurface.py`** — Core RCWA module (created from scratch)\n     - Physical constants: LAMBDA_NM=532, PERIOD_X=453, HEIGHT_NM=250, T_WG_MM=0.5, L_INCOUPLER_MM=3.0\n     - ZONE_BOUNDS_MM = [0.0, 1.06, 2.07, 3.00]\n     - N_BK7=1.5195, N_TIO2=complex(2.370, 0.001)\n     - REALISTIC_TARGETS = [(0.75,0.08), (0.52,0.35), (0.26,0.60)]\n     - Key function `_make_epsilon_grid(w_beam_nm, r_pillar_nm, x_pillar_nm, period_y_nm, Nx=200, Ny=50)`:\n       - Beam centered at x=0 (periodic), fills x∈[0, w/2]∪[PERIOD_X-w/2, PERIOD_X]\n       - Pillar: circle with periodic wrapping in both x and y using `np.round(dx/PERIOD_X)` trick\n     - Key function `run_rcwa(w_beam_nm, r_pillar_nm, x_pillar_nm, period_y_nm, theta_glass_deg, nG=51)`:\n       ```python\n       obj = grcwa.obj(nG, L1, L2, freq, theta=theta_rad, phi=0.0, verbose=0)\n       obj.Add_LayerUniform(0.0, N_BK7**2)   # substrate (incident)\n       obj.Add_LayerGrid(HEIGHT_NM, Nx, Ny)   # metasurface\n       obj.Add_LayerUniform(0.0, N_AIR**2+0j) # air (exit)\n       obj.Init_Setup(Gmethod=1)\n       obj.GridLayer_geteps(eps_flat)\n       obj.MakeExcitationPlanewave(p_amp=0, p_phase=0, s_amp=1, s_phase=0, order=0, direction=\"forward\")\n       R_arr, T_arr = obj.RT_Solve(normalize=1, byorder=1)\n       G = obj.G\n       idx_m10 = np.where((G[:,0]==-1) & (G[:,1]==0))[0]  # T[-1,0]\n       idx_00  = np.where((G[:,0]==0)  & (G[:,1]==0))[0]   # R[0,0]\n       ```\n     - `compute_coupling_efficiency()`: 1D ray-tracing model with n_x=2000 spatial samples\n       ```python\n       pitch = 2.0 * T_WG_MM * np.tan(tg_rad)  # TIR bounce pitch in mm\n       # For each x0: eta_c = eta_T(zone) * prod(eta_R(bounce zones))\n       ```\n     - TABLE1_PARAMS (final updated values after grid scanning):\n       ```python\n       TABLE1_PARAMS = [\n           [110.0, 50.0,  125.0, 350.0],   # Zone 1: w, r, xp, Ly\n           [110.0, 85.0,  160.0, 225.0],   # Zone 2 (best from scan)\n           [100.0, 98.0,  300.0, 200.0],   # Zone 3 (best from scan)\n       ]\n       ```\n     - Helper `_lambda_y_from_truncation(r_nm, w_p_nm)`: Λ_y = 2√(r²-(w_p/2)²)\n     - Derived: _LY2=66.4 nm, _LY3=112.5 nm (for reference only; actual Ly is from scan)\n\n   - **`/project/run_simulation.py`** — End-to-end runner (created from scratch)\n     - Stage 1: angle sweep with TABLE1_PARAMS initial geometry\n     - Stage 2: Nelder-Mead optimization per zone (maxiter=250, nG=51)\n     - Stage 3: select best between initial and optimized\n     - Stage 4: MFE calculation + plot generation\n     - Saves JSON results to `zone_results.json`\n     - Generates: `fig3_efficiency_curves.png`, `fig4_coupling_efficiency.png`, `zone_geometry.png`\n\n4. Fixed by:\n     1. Verifying nG convergence (nG=51 is sufficient, results stable at nG=75+)\n     2. Running systematic 2D grid scans over (x_p, Λ_y) pairs\n     3. Zone 2 best: x_p=160 nm, Ly=225 nm → η_T=48.6%, η_R=34.7%\n     4. This is a loss channel not captured in T+R[0,0] sum.\n   - **MFE bottleneck**: At XFOV=-10°, TIR pitch=0.874 mm. 250.0     # TiO2 nano-structure height, fixed for all zones (nm)\n35\tT_WG_MM    = 0.5       # waveguide thickness (mm)\n36\tL_INCOUPLER_MM = 3.0   # total in-coupler length (mm)\n37\t# Zone widths from Fig 2(e)\n38\tZONE_WIDTHS_MM = [1.06, 1.01, 0.93]\n39\t# Accumulated zone boundaries [0, 1.06, 2.07, 3.00] mm\n40\tZONE_BOUNDS_MM = [0.0] + list(np.cumsum(ZONE_WIDTHS_MM))\n41\t\n42\t\n43\t# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n44\t# Refractive indices\n45\t# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n46\tdef _n_nbk7(lam_um: float) -> float:\n47\t    \"\"\"N-BK7 Sellmeier equation, λ in µm.\"\"\"\n48\t    l2 = lam_um ** 2\n49\t    return float(np.sqrt(\n50\t        1\n51\t        + 1.03961212 * l2 / (l2 - 0.00600069867)\n52\t        + 0.231792344 * l2 / (l2 - 0.0200179144)\n53\t        + 1.01046945  * l2 / (l2 - 103.560653)\n54\t    ))\n55\t\n56\t\n57\tN_BK7  = _n_nbk7(LAMBDA_NM / 1000.0)   # ≈ 1.5195\n58\tN_TIO2 = complex(2.370, 0.001)         # ALD TiO2 at 532 nm (small imaginary for material loss)\n59\tN_AIR  = 1.0\n60\t\n61\t# Ideal per-zone diffraction targets from Fig 2(e) (sum = 100%)\n62\tIDEAL_TARGETS = [\n63\t    (0.96, 0.04),   # Zone 1: η_T, η_R\n64\t    (0.54, 0.46),   # Zone 2\n65\t    (0.27, 0.73),   # Zone 3\n66\t]\n67\t\n68\t# Adjusted realistic targets (sum < 100% due to absorption and other orders)\n69\t# Derived from Fig 3(d-f) simulation curves\n70\tREALISTIC_TARGETS = [\n71\t    (0.75, 0.08),   # Zone 1\n72\t    (0.52, 0.35),   # Zone 2\n73\t    (0.26, 0.60),   # Zone 3\n74\t]\n75\t\n76\t\n77\t# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n78\t# Angle conversions  (grating equation, m = +1 into glass)\n79\t# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n80\tdef xfov_to_glass(theta_air_deg: float) -> float:\n81\t    \"\"\"XFOV (air) → equivalent glass angle via +1-order grating equation.\"\"\"\n82\t    s = (np.sin(np.radians(theta_air_deg)) + LAMBDA_NM / PERIOD_X) / N_BK7\n83\t    return float(np.degrees(np.arcsin(np.clip(s, -1, 1))))\n84\t\n85\t\n86\tdef glass_to_xfov(theta_glass_deg: float) -> float:\n87\t    \"\"\"Glass angle → XFOV (air) via −1-order grating equation.\"\"\"\n88\t    s = N_BK7 * np.sin(np.radians(theta_glass_deg)) - LAMBDA_NM / PERIOD_X\n89\t    return float(np.degrees(np.arcsin(np.clip(s, -1, 1))))\n90\t\n91\t\n92\t# XFOV range and corresponding glass angles\n93\tXFOV_DEG    = np.linspace(-10.0, 10.0, 21)\n94\tTHETA_GLASS = np.array([xfov_to_glass(t) for t in XFOV_DEG])\n95\t\n96\t\n97\t# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n98\t# Geometry helpers\n99\t# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n100\tdef _make_epsilon_grid(\n101\t    w_beam_nm:    float,   # beam (bar) width in x\n102\t    r_pillar_nm:  float,   # pillar radius\n103\t    x_pillar_nm:  float,   # pillar centre x  (can be > PERIOD_X/2, handled by PBC)\n104\t    period_y_nm:  float,   # y unit-cell size\n105\t    Nx: int = 200,\n106\t    Ny: int = 50,\n107\t) -> np.ndarray:\n108\t    \"\"\"\n109\t    Return flattened Nx*Ny epsilon array for one metasurface unit cell.\n110\t\n111\t    x ∈ [0, PERIOD_X],  y ∈ [0, period_y].\n112\t    Nano-beam  : rectangle of width w_beam centred at x=0 (periodic boundary\n113\t                 ensures wrapping: occupies x ∈ [0, w/2] ∪ [Px−w/2, Px]).\n114\t    Nano-pillar: circle of radius r_p centred at (x_p, period_y/2).\n115\t                 If x_p + r_p > PERIOD_X the circle wraps around x; grcwa\n116\t                 handles this naturally via Fourier periodicity once the grid\n117\t                 is filled with wrapped coordinates.\n118\t    \"\"\"\n119\t    eps_tio2 = N_TIO2 ** 2\n120\t    eps_air  = N_AIR  ** 2\n121\t\n122\t    x = np.linspace(0, PERIOD_X,  Nx, endpoint=False)\n123\t    y = np.linspace(0, period_y_nm, Ny, endpoint=False)\n124\t    XX, YY = np.meshgrid(x, y, indexing=\"ij\")   # shape (Nx, Ny)\n125\t\n126\t    grid = np.full((Nx, Ny), eps_air, dtype=complex)\n127\t\n128\t    # ── nano-beam: centred at x = 0, full y-extent ──────────────────────────\n129\t    half = w_beam_nm / 2.0\n130\t    beam_mask = (XX <= half) | (XX >= PERIOD_X - half)\n131\t    grid[beam_mask] = eps_tio2\n132\t\n133\t    # ── nano-pillar: centred at (x_p, period_y/2), periodic in x ─────────\n134\t    cy = period_y_nm / 2.0\n135\t    # Compute wrapped x-distance to handle circular periodicity\n136\t    dx = XX - x_pillar_nm\n137\t    dx_wrap = dx - PERIOD_X * np.round(dx / PERIOD_X)   # nearest-image in x\n138\t    dy = YY - cy\n139\t    # Also wrap in y (though usually period_y > 2*r_p for zone 1;\n140\t    # zones 2/3 may have pillar at x-boundary only)\n141\t    dy_wrap = dy - period_y_nm * np.round(dy / period_y_nm)\n142\t    dist = np.sqrt(dx_wrap ** 2 + dy_wrap ** 2)\n143\t    grid[dist < r_pillar_nm] = eps_tio2\n144\t\n145\t    return grid.flatten()\n146\t\n147\t\n148\t# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n149\t# Core grcwa RCWA simulation\n150\t# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n151\tdef run_rcwa(\n152\t    w_beam_nm:   float,\n153\t    r_pillar_nm: float,\n154\t    x_pillar_nm: float,\n155\t    period_y_nm: float,\n156\t    theta_glass_deg: float,\n157\t    nG: int = 51,\n158\t    Nx: int = 200,\n159\t    Ny: int = 50,\n160\t) -> tuple:\n161\t    \"\"\"\n162\t    Single-angle grcwa RCWA call.\n163\t\n164\t    Source: TE plane wave from glass substrate at polar angle theta_glass_deg.\n165\t    Stack (front → back in grcwa): glass | metasurface | air.\n166\t\n167\t    Returns\n168\t    -------\n169\t    T_m1 : float  – η_{T,−1}(glass) = η_{T,+1}(air), first-order diffraction\n170\t    R_0  : float  – η_{R,0}(glass), zeroth-order reflection\n171\t    \"\"\"\n172\t    freq = 1.0 / LAMBDA_NM          # in 1/nm (lengths in nm throughout)\n173\t    theta_rad = np.radians(theta_glass_deg)\n174\t\n175\t    L1 = [PERIOD_X,    0.0        ]\n176\t    L2 = [0.0,         period_y_nm]\n177\t\n178\t    obj = grcwa.obj(nG, L1, L2, freq,\n179\t                    theta=theta_rad, phi=0.0, verbose=0)\n180\t\n181\t    # Layers: glass (incident) → patterned metasurface → air (exit)\n182\t    obj.Add_LayerUniform(0.0,       N_BK7  ** 2)          # substrate (semi-∞)\n183\t    obj.Add_LayerGrid   (HEIGHT_NM, Nx, Ny)               # metasurface\n184\t    obj.Add_LayerUniform(0.0,       N_AIR  ** 2 + 0j)     # air (semi-∞)\n185\t\n186\t    obj.Init_Setup(Gmethod=1)    # rectangular G-vector truncation\n187\t\n188\t    eps_flat = _make_epsilon_grid(w_beam_nm, r_pillar_nm,\n189\t                                  x_pillar_nm, period_y_nm, Nx, Ny)\n190\t    obj.GridLayer_geteps(eps_flat)\n191\t\n192\t    # TE excitation from glass (s-amplitude = 1, p = 0)\n193\t    obj.MakeExcitationPlanewave(p_amp=0, p_phase=0,\n194\t                                s_amp=1, s_phase=0,\n195\t                                order=0, direction=\"forward\")\n196\t\n197\t    R_arr, T_arr = obj.RT_Solve(normalize=1, byorder=1)\n198\t\n199\t    G = obj.G  # shape (nG_actual, 2)\n200\t\n201\t    # η_{T,−1,0}: (m,n) = (−1, 0) order in air exit layer\n202\t    idx_m10 = np.where((G[:, 0] == -1) & (G[:, 1] == 0))[0]\n203\t    T_m1 = float(T_arr[idx_m10[0]]) if len(idx_m10) else 0.0\n204\t\n205\t    # η_{R,0,0}: (0,0) order in glass input layer\n206\t    idx_00 = np.where((G[:, 0] == 0) & (G[:, 1] == 0))[0]\n207\t    R_0 = float(R_arr[idx_00[0]]) if len(idx_00) else 0.0\n208\t\n209\t    # Clamp small numerical negatives\n210\t    T_m1 = max(T_m1, 0.0)\n211\t    R_0  = max(R_0,  0.0)\n212\t    return T_m1, R_0\n213\t\n214\t\n215\t# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n216\t# Angle sweep for one zone\n217\t# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n218\tdef angle_sweep(\n219\t    params: tuple,\n220\t    theta_glass_arr: np.ndarray = THETA_GLASS,\n221\t    **rcwa_kw,\n222\t) -> tuple:\n223\t    \"\"\"\n224\t    Run RCWA at each angle in theta_glass_arr.\n225\t    params = (w_beam_nm, r_pillar_nm, x_pillar_nm, period_y_nm).\n226\t    Returns (T_arr, R_arr) each shape (n_angles,).\n227\t    \"\"\"\n228\t    w, r, xp, ly = params\n229\t    T_list, R_list = [], []\n230\t    for tg in theta_glass_arr:\n231\t        T, R = run_rcwa(w, r, xp, ly, tg, **rcwa_kw)\n232\t        T_list.append(T)\n233\t        R_list.append(R)\n234\t    return np.array(T_list), np.array(R_list)\n235\t\n236\t\n237\t# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n238\t# Optimisation merit function\n239\t# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n240\tdef _merit(x, target_T, target_R, theta_glass_arr, zone_id, **rcwa_kw):\n241\t    \"\"\"\n242\t    MSE between simulated average efficiencies and targets.\n243\t    x = [w_beam, r_pillar, x_pillar, period_y]  (all in nm, un-bounded).\n244\t    \"\"\"\n245\t    w, r, xp, ly = x\n246\t    # Hard constraints\n247\t    if w < 20 or w > 300 or r < 10 or r > 250 or xp < 10 or ly < 50:\n248\t        return 1e6\n249\t    if xp > PERIOD_X + r:   # pillar entirely outside cell even with wrap\n250\t        return 1e6\n251\t\n252\t    T_arr, R_arr = angle_sweep((w, r, xp, ly), theta_glass_arr, **rcwa_kw)\n253\t\n254\t    # Zone 1: balance across full XFOV (weight both ends equally)\n255\t    # Zones 2, 3: optimised primarily for left end of XFOV (−10°)\n256\t    if zone_id == 0:\n257\t        # Average over full XFOV with extra weight on edges\n258\t        weights = np.ones(len(T_arr))\n259\t        weights[0]  = 3.0   # −10° end\n260\t        weights[-1] = 2.0   # +10° end\n261\t        weights /= weights.sum()\n262\t    else:\n263\t        # Weight strongly toward −10° (left end, small glass angle)\n264\t        weights = np.ones(len(T_arr))\n265\t        weights[0] = 5.0\n266\t        weights /= weights.sum()\n267\t\n268\t    T_mean = float(np.dot(weights, T_arr))\n269\t    R_mean = float(np.dot(weights, R_arr))\n270\t\n271\t    loss = (T_mean - target_T) ** 2 + (R_mean - target_R) ** 2\n272\t    return loss\n273\t\n274\t\n275\tdef optimize_zone(\n276\t    zone_id:  int,\n277\t    x0:       list,\n278\t    target_T: float,\n279\t    target_R: float,\n280\t    bounds:   list = None,\n281\t    method:   str  = \"Nelder-Mead\",\n282\t    maxiter:  int  = 300,\n283\t    **rcwa_kw,\n284\t) -> dict:\n285\t    \"\"\"\n286\t    Optimise geometry for a single zone.\n287\t\n288\t    Parameters\n289\t    ----------\n290\t    zone_id  : 0, 1, or 2\n291\t    x0       : [w_beam, r_pillar, x_pillar, period_y] initial guess (nm)\n292\t    target_T : target first-order diffraction efficiency (0–1)\n293\t    target_R : target zeroth-order reflection efficiency (0–1)\n294\t    bounds   : list of (lo, hi) for each variable\n295\t    method   : scipy.optimize method\n296\t    \"\"\"\n297\t    theta_arr = THETA_GLASS\n298\t\n299\t    def merit(x):\n300\t        return _merit(x, target_T, target_R, theta_arr, zone_id, **rcwa_kw)\n301\t\n302\t    options = {\"maxiter\": maxiter, \"xatol\": 1.0, \"fatol\": 1e-4}\n303\t\n304\t    if method == \"differential_evolution\":\n305\t        if bounds is None:\n306\t            raise ValueError(\"bounds required for differential_evolution\")\n307\t        result = differential_evolution(merit, bounds, maxiter=maxiter,\n308\t                                        tol=1e-4, seed=42, workers=1)\n309\t    else:\n310\t        result = minimize(merit, x0, method=method, options=options)\n311\t\n312\t    best = result.x\n313\t    T_arr, R_arr = angle_sweep(tuple(best), theta_arr, **rcwa_kw)\n314\t    return {\n315\t        \"params\":  best.tolist(),\n316\t        \"T_array\": T_arr.tolist(),\n317\t        \"R_array\": R_arr.tolist(),\n318\t        \"loss\":    float(result.fun),\n319\t        \"success\": result.success,\n320\t    }\n321\t\n322\t\n323\t# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n324\t# MFE  — 1D ray-tracing model\n325\t# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n326\tdef compute_coupling_efficiency(\n327\t    T_zones:  list,    # [T_arr_zone0, T_arr_zone1, T_arr_zone2]  shape (n_ang,)\n328\t    R_zones:  list,    # [R_arr_zone0, …]\n329\t    xfov_deg: np.ndarray = XFOV_DEG,\n330\t    n_x:      int = 2000,\n331\t) -> np.ndarray:\n332\t    \"\"\"\n333\t    Compute coupling efficiency η_c(θ_i) using a 1-D ray-tracing model.\n334\t\n335\t    For a ray entering at position x_0 in the in-coupler [0, L_total]:\n336\t      • Primary coupling: η_T(zone at x_0)  — fraction entering waveguide.\n337\t      • The trapped beam travels at angle θ_glass; TIR pitch = 2t·tan(θ_glass).\n338\t      • Each time it hits the in-coupler from below: it retains η_R(zone at x_n)\n339\t        of its power (0th-order reflection); the rest diffracts back out (loss).\n340\t    η_c(x_0) = η_T(x_0) · ∏_{n=1}^{N} η_R(x_n)   where x_n = x_0 + n·pitch,\n341\t               N = max n such that x_n < L_total.\n342\t    η_c_total(θ_i) = mean over x_0 ∈ [0, L_total].\n343\t\n344\t    Returns array of coupling efficiencies, shape (n_angles,).\n345\t    \"\"\"\n346\t    L      = ZONE_BOUNDS_MM[-1]                # 3.0 mm\n347\t    bounds = ZONE_BOUNDS_MM                    # [0, 1.06, 2.07, 3.0]\n348\t    x0     = np.linspace(0, L, n_x, endpoint=False)\n349\t    n_zones = len(ZONE_WIDTHS_MM)\n350\t\n351\t    coupling = []\n352\t    for ai, theta_air in enumerate(xfov_deg):\n353\t        # Glass angle and TIR pitch (in mm)\n354\t        tg_rad = np.radians(xfov_to_glass(theta_air))\n355\t        pitch  = 2.0 * T_WG_MM * np.tan(tg_rad)   # mm\n356\t\n357\t        # Zone index for each starting position\n358\t        zone_of = np.searchsorted(bounds[1:], x0)\n359\t        zone_of = np.clip(zone_of, 0, n_zones - 1)\n360\t\n361\t        # Primary coupling: η_T of the zone at x0\n362\t        eta_c = np.array([T_zones[z][ai] for z in zone_of], dtype=float)\n363\t\n364\t        # Secondary interactions: multiply by η_R for each bounce inside coupler\n365\t        # Vectorised over bounces; max bounces = ceil(L/pitch)\n366\t        if pitch > 0:\n367\t            max_bounces = int(np.ceil(L / pitch)) + 1\n368\t        else:\n369\t            max_bounces = 0\n370\t\n371\t        for n in range(1, max_bounces + 1):\n372\t            xn = x0 + n * pitch\n373\t            inside = xn < L\n374\t            if not np.any(inside):\n375\t                break\n376\t            zn = np.searchsorted(bounds[1:], xn[inside])\n377\t            zn = np.clip(zn, 0, n_zones - 1)\n378\t            r_vals = np.array([R_zones[z][ai] for z in zn], dtype=float)\n379\t            eta_c[inside] *= r_vals\n380\t\n381\t        coupling.append(float(np.mean(eta_c)))\n382\t\n383\t    return np.array(coupling)\n384\t\n385\t\n386\tdef compute_mfe(T_zones, R_zones, xfov_deg=XFOV_DEG):\n387\t    \"\"\"Return (mfe, coupling_array).\"\"\"\n388\t    coupling = compute_coupling_efficiency(T_zones, R_zones, xfov_deg)\n389\t    mfe = float(np.min(coupling))\n390\t    return mfe, coupling\n391\t\n392\t\n393\t# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n394\t# Plotting\n395\t# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n396\tdef plot_efficiency_curves(\n397\t    T_zones:   list,\n398\t    R_zones:   list,\n399\t    xfov_deg:  np.ndarray = XFOV_DEG,\n400\t    params:    list = None,\n401\t    out_path:  str = \"fig3_efficiency_curves.png\",\n402\t):\n403\t    \"\"\"Reproduce Fig 3(d-f): efficiency vs incident angle for all 3 zones.\"\"\"\n404\t    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(14, 4), sharey=True)\n405\t\n406\t    sec_ax_labels = [f\"{xfov_to_glass(t):.1f}\" for t in xfov_deg[::5]]\n407\t    sec_ax_ticks  = xfov_deg[::5]\n408\t\n409\t    for k, ax in enumerate(axes):\n410\t        T = np.array(T_zones[k]) * 100\n411\t        R = np.array(R_zones[k]) * 100\n412\t        S = T + R\n413\t\n414\t        ax.plot(xfov_deg, T, \"r-o\",  ms=4, lw=1.5, label=r\"$\\eta_{T,+1}$ (sim)\")\n415\t        ax.plot(xfov_deg, R, \"b-s\",  ms=4, lw=1.5, label=r\"$\\eta_{R,0}$ (sim)\")\n416\t        ax.plot(xfov_deg, S, \"k--\",  ms=3, lw=1.0, label=\"Sum\")\n417\t\n418\t        if params is not None:\n419\t            w, r, xp, ly = params[k]\n420\t            ax.set_title(\n421\t                f\"Zone f\"TiO₂/N-BK7 Metasurface In-Coupler — Efficiency Curves\\n\"\n446\t        f\"λ=532 nm, period={PERIOD_X:.0f} nm, height={HEIGHT_NM:.0f} nm\",\n447\t        fontsize=10,\n448\t    )\n449\t    fig.tight_layout()\n450\t    fig.savefig(out_path, dpi=150, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\n451\t    plt.close(fig)\n452\t    print(f\"Saved: {out_path}\")\n453\t\n454\t\n455\tdef plot_mfe_curve(\n456\t epsilon map for each zone unit cell.\n485\t    \"\"\"\n486\t    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(12, 4))\n487\t    for k, ax in enumerate(axes):\n488\t        w, r, xp, ly = )\n498\t        ax.set_title(\n499\t            f\"Zone {k+1}\\nw={w:.0f}, d_p={2*r:.0f}, x_p={xp:.0f}, Λ_y={ly:.0f} nm\",\n500\t            fontsize=8,\n501\t        )\n502\t        ax.set_xlabel(\"x ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n513\t# Default starting geometry (from Table 1, with estimated x_p / period_y)\n514\t# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n515\t#\n516\t# Geometry interpretation (confirmed by SEM in Fig 5 and paper text):\n517\t#   • The grating period is in x (Λ_x = 453 nm).\n518\t#   • Each unit cell has a nano-beam (running the full y-extent) and a\n519\t#     nano-pillar (circular cylinder).\n520\t#   • For zones 2 & 3 the pillar diameter d_p EXCEEDS Λ_y, so the pillar is\n521\t#     truncated at the y-boundaries, appearing as a \"partial circle\" in SEM.\n522\t#   • \"Pillar width\" w_p (Table 1) = chord length in x at the y=0 boundary:\n523\t#       w_p = 2·√(r_p² − (Λ_y/2)²)  →  Λ_y = 2·√(r_p² − (w_p/2)²)\n524\t#   • x_p (pillar centre x) is between beam right edge and PERIOD_X − beam_half,\n525\t#     estimated from figures (~200 nm from left edge for zones 2/3).\n526\t\n527\tdef _lambda_y_from_truncation(r_nm: float, w_p_nm: float) -> float:\n528\t    \"\"\"Λ_y given pillar radius r_p and chord width w_p at y-boundary.\"\"\"\n529\t    val = r_nm**2 - (w_p_nm / 2.0)**2\n530\t    return float(2.0 * np.sqrt(max(val, 0.0)))\n531\t\n532\tdef _x_pillar_from_truncation(r_nm, w_p_nm):\n533\t    \"\"\"Legacy: x_p if truncation were in x direction (not used for Table-1 params).\"\"\"\n534\t    delta = float(np.sqrt(max(r_nm**2 - (w_p_nm/2)**2, 0)))\n535\t    return PERIOD_X - delta\n536\t\n537\t\n538\t# Λ_y for zones 2 & 3 from y-truncation condition (Table 1 designed values)\n539\t_LY2 = _lambda_y_from_truncation(85.0,  156.5)   # zone 2: ~66.4 nm\n540\t_LY3 = _lambda_y_from_truncation(98.0,  160.5)   # zone 3: ~112.4 nm\n541\t\n542\tTABLE1_PARAMS = [\n543\t    # Zone 1: full circle (no truncation); x_p ≈ 125 nm (gap~20 nm), Λ_y ≈ 350 nm\n544\t    #   Table 1 design: w_b=110, d_p=100 — gives η_T~87%, η_R~6% at XFOV=-10°\n545\t    [110.0, 50.0,  125.0, 350.0],\n546\t    # Zone 2: Table 1 design: w_b=110, d_p=170\n547\t    #   Best x_p/Ly from grid scan targeting η_T~54%, η_R~46%\n548\t    #   xp=160, Ly=225 → η_T=48.6%, η_R=34.7% at XFOV=-10°  (R[-1,0]=14%)\n549\t    [110.0, 85.0,  160.0, 225.0],\n550\t    # Zone 3: Table 1 design: w_b=100, d_p=196\n551\t    #   Best x_p/Ly from grid scan targeting η_T~27%, η_R~73%\n552\t    #   xp=300, Ly=200 → η_T=25.7%, η_R=55.9% at XFOV=-10°  (R[-1,0]=12.3%)\n553\t    [100.0, 98.0,  300.0, 200.0],\n554\t]\n555\t\n556\t# Optimisation bounds: [w_beam, r_pillar, x_pillar, period_y]\n557\tOPT_BOUNDS = [\n558\t    [(60,  200), (20, 120), ( 60, 380), (150, 600)],   # Zone 1\n559\t    [(60,  200), (50, 130), ( 80, 380), (100, 400)],   # Zone 2\n560\t    [(60,  200), (60, 150), (150, 400), (100, 400)],   # Zone 3\n561\t]\n562\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c5e4dbe4-1f62-4db3-8732-1c178dbf347a","uuid":"1d5eea28-c5c9-4933-ac91-7e6dc3135c47","timestamp":"2026-06-08T22:18:53.928Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/shrutibadhwar/Documents/2026/testpackage/sciagent-bench/results/20260608T200907Z/photonics/photonics__cc-bare__sonnet/project/rcwa_metasurface.py","content":"\"\"\"\nTiO2/N-BK7 three-zone metasurface in-coupler RCWA simulation.\n\nReproduces the design from:\n  \"Design and experimental validation of a high-efficiency multi-zone\n   metasurface waveguide in-coupler\", Optical Materials Express, 2025.\n\nKey design choices (Table 1 / Fig 3):\n  - λ = 532 nm, grating period = 453 nm, TiO2 height = 250 nm (shared)\n  - Three zones: widths 1.06 / 1.01 / 0.93 mm\n  - Per-zone targets (Fig 2e): η_T = 96/54/27%, η_R = 4/46/73%\n  - Simulation: TE-polarised source from N-BK7 glass, angle range 41–63°\n    (equivalent to XFOV −10° to +10° by light-reversibility)\n\"\"\"\n\nimport warnings\nwarnings.filterwarnings(\"ignore\")\n\nimport numpy as np\nimport matplotlib\nmatplotlib.use(\"Agg\")\nimport matplotlib.pyplot as plt\nfrom scipy.optimize import minimize, differential_evolution\nimport json\nimport os\n\nimport grcwa\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Physical constants\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nLAMBDA_NM  = 532.0     # wavelength (nm)\nPERIOD_X   = 453.0     # grating period in x (nm)\nHEIGHT_NM  = 250.0     # TiO2 nano-structure height, fixed for all zones (nm)\nT_WG_MM    = 0.5       # waveguide thickness (mm)\nL_INCOUPLER_MM = 3.0   # total in-coupler length (mm)\n# Zone widths from Fig 2(e)\nZONE_WIDTHS_MM = [1.06, 1.01, 0.93]\n# Accumulated zone boundaries [0, 1.06, 2.07, 3.00] mm\nZONE_BOUNDS_MM = [0.0] + list(np.cumsum(ZONE_WIDTHS_MM))\n\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Refractive indices\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef _n_nbk7(lam_um: float) -> float:\n    \"\"\"N-BK7 Sellmeier equation, λ in µm.\"\"\"\n    l2 = lam_um ** 2\n    return float(np.sqrt(\n        1\n        + 1.03961212 * l2 / (l2 - 0.00600069867)\n        + 0.231792344 * l2 / (l2 - 0.0200179144)\n        + 1.01046945  * l2 / (l2 - 103.560653)\n    ))\n\n\nN_BK7  = _n_nbk7(LAMBDA_NM / 1000.0)   # ≈ 1.5195\nN_TIO2 = complex(2.370, 0.001)         # ALD TiO2 at 532 nm (small imaginary for material loss)\nN_AIR  = 1.0\n\n# Ideal per-zone diffraction targets from Fig 2(e) (sum = 100%)\nIDEAL_TARGETS = [\n    (0.96, 0.04),   # Zone 1: η_T, η_R\n    (0.54, 0.46),   # Zone 2\n    (0.27, 0.73),   # Zone 3\n]\n\n# Adjusted realistic targets (sum < 100% due to absorption and other orders)\n# Derived from Fig 3(d-f) simulation curves\nREALISTIC_TARGETS = [\n    (0.75, 0.08),   # Zone 1\n    (0.52, 0.35),   # Zone 2\n    (0.26, 0.60),   # Zone 3\n]\n\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Angle conversions  (grating equation, m = +1 into glass)\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef xfov_to_glass(theta_air_deg: float) -> float:\n    \"\"\"XFOV (air) → equivalent glass angle via +1-order grating equation.\"\"\"\n    s = (np.sin(np.radians(theta_air_deg)) + LAMBDA_NM / PERIOD_X) / N_BK7\n    return float(np.degrees(np.arcsin(np.clip(s, -1, 1))))\n\n\ndef glass_to_xfov(theta_glass_deg: float) -> float:\n    \"\"\"Glass angle → XFOV (air) via −1-order grating equation.\"\"\"\n    s = N_BK7 * np.sin(np.radians(theta_glass_deg)) - LAMBDA_NM / PERIOD_X\n    return float(np.degrees(np.arcsin(np.clip(s, -1, 1))))\n\n\n# XFOV range and corresponding glass angles\nXFOV_DEG    = np.linspace(-10.0, 10.0, 21)\nTHETA_GLASS = np.array([xfov_to_glass(t) for t in XFOV_DEG])\n\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Geometry helpers\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef _make_epsilon_grid(\n    w_beam_nm:    float,   # beam (bar) width in x\n    r_pillar_nm:  float,   # pillar radius\n    x_pillar_nm:  float,   # pillar centre x  (can be > PERIOD_X/2, handled by PBC)\n    period_y_nm:  float,   # y unit-cell size\n    Nx: int = 200,\n    Ny: int = 50,\n) -> np.ndarray:\n    \"\"\"\n    Return flattened Nx*Ny epsilon array for one metasurface unit cell.\n\n    x ∈ [0, PERIOD_X],  y ∈ [0, period_y].\n    Nano-beam  : rectangle of width w_beam centred at x=0 (periodic boundary\n                 ensures wrapping: occupies x ∈ [0, w/2] ∪ [Px−w/2, Px]).\n    Nano-pillar: circle of radius r_p centred at (x_p, period_y/2).\n                 If x_p + r_p > PERIOD_X the circle wraps around x; grcwa\n                 handles this naturally via Fourier periodicity once the grid\n                 is filled with wrapped coordinates.\n    \"\"\"\n    eps_tio2 = N_TIO2 ** 2\n    eps_air  = N_AIR  ** 2\n\n    x = np.linspace(0, PERIOD_X,  Nx, endpoint=False)\n    y = np.linspace(0, period_y_nm, Ny, endpoint=False)\n    XX, YY = np.meshgrid(x, y, indexing=\"ij\")   # shape (Nx, Ny)\n\n    grid = np.full((Nx, Ny), eps_air, dtype=complex)\n\n    # ── nano-beam: centred at x = 0, full y-extent ──────────────────────────\n    half = w_beam_nm / 2.0\n    beam_mask = (XX <= half) | (XX >= PERIOD_X - half)\n    grid[beam_mask] = eps_tio2\n\n    # ── nano-pillar: centred at (x_p, period_y/2), periodic in x ─────────\n    cy = period_y_nm / 2.0\n    # Compute wrapped x-distance to handle circular periodicity\n    dx = XX - x_pillar_nm\n    dx_wrap = dx - PERIOD_X * np.round(dx / PERIOD_X)   # nearest-image in x\n    dy = YY - cy\n    # Also wrap in y (though usually period_y > 2*r_p for zone 1;\n    # zones 2/3 may have pillar at x-boundary only)\n    dy_wrap = dy - period_y_nm * np.round(dy / period_y_nm)\n    dist = np.sqrt(dx_wrap ** 2 + dy_wrap ** 2)\n    grid[dist < r_pillar_nm] = eps_tio2\n\n    return grid.flatten()\n\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Core grcwa RCWA simulation\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef run_rcwa(\n    w_beam_nm:   float,\n    r_pillar_nm: float,\n    x_pillar_nm: float,\n    period_y_nm: float,\n    theta_glass_deg: float,\n    nG: int = 51,\n    Nx: int = 200,\n    Ny: int = 50,\n) -> tuple:\n    \"\"\"\n    Single-angle grcwa RCWA call.\n\n    Source: TE plane wave from glass substrate at polar angle theta_glass_deg.\n    Stack (front → back in grcwa): glass | metasurface | air.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    T_m1 : float  – η_{T,−1}(glass) = η_{T,+1}(air), first-order diffraction\n    R_0  : float  – η_{R,0}(glass), zeroth-order reflection\n    \"\"\"\n    freq = 1.0 / LAMBDA_NM          # in 1/nm (lengths in nm throughout)\n    theta_rad = np.radians(theta_glass_deg)\n\n    L1 = [PERIOD_X,    0.0        ]\n    L2 = [0.0,         period_y_nm]\n\n    obj = grcwa.obj(nG, L1, L2, freq,\n                    theta=theta_rad, phi=0.0, verbose=0)\n\n    # Layers: glass (incident) → patterned metasurface → air (exit)\n    obj.Add_LayerUniform(0.0,       N_BK7  ** 2)          # substrate (semi-∞)\n    obj.Add_LayerGrid   (HEIGHT_NM, Nx, Ny)               # metasurface\n    obj.Add_LayerUniform(0.0,       N_AIR  ** 2 + 0j)     # air (semi-∞)\n\n    obj.Init_Setup(Gmethod=1)    # rectangular G-vector truncation\n\n    eps_flat = _make_epsilon_grid(w_beam_nm, r_pillar_nm,\n                                  x_pillar_nm, period_y_nm, Nx, Ny)\n    obj.GridLayer_geteps(eps_flat)\n\n    # TE excitation from glass (s-amplitude = 1, p = 0)\n    obj.MakeExcitationPlanewave(p_amp=0, p_phase=0,\n                                s_amp=1, s_phase=0,\n                                order=0, direction=\"forward\")\n\n    R_arr, T_arr = obj.RT_Solve(normalize=1, byorder=1)\n\n    G = obj.G  # shape (nG_actual, 2)\n\n    # η_{T,−1,0}: (m,n) = (−1, 0) order in air exit layer\n    idx_m10 = np.where((G[:, 0] == -1) & (G[:, 1] == 0))[0]\n    T_m1 = float(T_arr[idx_m10[0]]) if len(idx_m10) else 0.0\n\n    # η_{R,0,0}: (0,0) order in glass input layer\n    idx_00 = np.where((G[:, 0] == 0) & (G[:, 1] == 0))[0]\n    R_0 = float(R_arr[idx_00[0]]) if len(idx_00) else 0.0\n\n    # Clamp small numerical negatives\n    T_m1 = max(T_m1, 0.0)\n    R_0  = max(R_0,  0.0)\n    return T_m1, R_0\n\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Angle sweep for one zone\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef angle_sweep(\n    params: tuple,\n    theta_glass_arr: np.ndarray = THETA_GLASS,\n    **rcwa_kw,\n) -> tuple:\n    \"\"\"\n    Run RCWA at each angle in theta_glass_arr.\n    params = (w_beam_nm, r_pillar_nm, x_pillar_nm, period_y_nm).\n    Returns (T_arr, R_arr) each shape (n_angles,).\n    \"\"\"\n    w, r, xp, ly = params\n    T_list, R_list = [], []\n    for tg in theta_glass_arr:\n        T, R = run_rcwa(w, r, xp, ly, tg, **rcwa_kw)\n        T_list.append(T)\n        R_list.append(R)\n    return np.array(T_list), np.array(R_list)\n\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Optimisation merit function\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef _merit(x, target_T, target_R, theta_glass_arr, zone_id, **rcwa_kw):\n    \"\"\"\n    MSE between simulated average efficiencies and targets.\n    x = [w_beam, r_pillar, x_pillar, period_y]  (all in nm, un-bounded).\n    \"\"\"\n    w, r, xp, ly = x\n    # Hard constraints\n    if w < 20 or w > 300 or r < 10 or r > 250 or xp < 10 or ly < 50:\n        return 1e6\n    if xp > PERIOD_X + r:   # pillar entirely outside cell even with wrap\n        return 1e6\n\n    T_arr, R_arr = angle_sweep((w, r, xp, ly), theta_glass_arr, **rcwa_kw)\n\n    # Zone 1: balance across full XFOV (weight both ends equally)\n    # Zones 2, 3: optimised primarily for left end of XFOV (−10°)\n    if zone_id == 0:\n        # Average over full XFOV with extra weight on edges\n        weights = np.ones(len(T_arr))\n        weights[0]  = 3.0   # −10° end\n        weights[-1] = 2.0   # +10° end\n        weights /= weights.sum()\n    else:\n        # Weight strongly toward −10° (left end, small glass angle)\n        weights = np.ones(len(T_arr))\n        weights[0] = 5.0\n        weights /= weights.sum()\n\n    T_mean = float(np.dot(weights, T_arr))\n    R_mean = float(np.dot(weights, R_arr))\n\n    loss = (T_mean - target_T) ** 2 + (R_mean - target_R) ** 2\n    return loss\n\n\ndef optimize_zone(\n    zone_id:  int,\n    x0:       list,\n    target_T: float,\n    target_R: float,\n    bounds:   list = None,\n    method:   str  = \"Nelder-Mead\",\n    maxiter:  int  = 300,\n    **rcwa_kw,\n) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"\n    Optimise geometry for a single zone.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    zone_id  : 0, 1, or 2\n    x0       : [w_beam, r_pillar, x_pillar, period_y] initial guess (nm)\n    target_T : target first-order diffraction efficiency (0–1)\n    target_R : target zeroth-order reflection efficiency (0–1)\n    bounds   : list of (lo, hi) for each variable\n    method   : scipy.optimize method\n    \"\"\"\n    theta_arr = THETA_GLASS\n\n    def merit(x):\n        return _merit(x, target_T, target_R, theta_arr, zone_id, **rcwa_kw)\n\n    options = {\"maxiter\": maxiter, \"xatol\": 1.0, \"fatol\": 1e-4}\n\n    if method == \"differential_evolution\":\n        if bounds is None:\n            raise ValueError(\"bounds required for differential_evolution\")\n        result = differential_evolution(merit, bounds, maxiter=maxiter,\n                                        tol=1e-4, seed=42, workers=1)\n    else:\n        result = minimize(merit, x0, method=method, options=options)\n\n    best = result.x\n    T_arr, R_arr = angle_sweep(tuple(best), theta_arr, **rcwa_kw)\n    return {\n        \"params\":  best.tolist(),\n        \"T_array\": T_arr.tolist(),\n        \"R_array\": R_arr.tolist(),\n        \"loss\":    float(result.fun),\n        \"success\": result.success,\n    }\n\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# MFE  — 1D ray-tracing model\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef compute_coupling_efficiency(\n    T_zones:  list,    # [T_arr_zone0, T_arr_zone1, T_arr_zone2]  shape (n_ang,)\n    R_zones:  list,    # [R_arr_zone0, …]\n    xfov_deg: np.ndarray = XFOV_DEG,\n    n_x:      int = 2000,\n) -> np.ndarray:\n    \"\"\"\n    Compute coupling efficiency η_c(θ_i) using a 1-D ray-tracing model.\n\n    For a ray entering at position x_0 in the in-coupler [0, L_total]:\n      • Primary coupling: η_T(zone at x_0)  — fraction entering waveguide.\n      • The trapped beam travels at angle θ_glass; TIR pitch = 2t·tan(θ_glass).\n      • Each time it hits the in-coupler from below: it retains η_R(zone at x_n)\n        of its power (0th-order reflection); the rest diffracts back out (loss).\n    η_c(x_0) = η_T(x_0) · ∏_{n=1}^{N} η_R(x_n)   where x_n = x_0 + n·pitch,\n               N = max n such that x_n < L_total.\n    η_c_total(θ_i) = mean over x_0 ∈ [0, L_total].\n\n    Returns array of coupling efficiencies, shape (n_angles,).\n    \"\"\"\n    L      = ZONE_BOUNDS_MM[-1]                # 3.0 mm\n    bounds = ZONE_BOUNDS_MM                    # [0, 1.06, 2.07, 3.0]\n    x0     = np.linspace(0, L, n_x, endpoint=False)\n    n_zones = len(ZONE_WIDTHS_MM)\n\n    coupling = []\n    for ai, theta_air in enumerate(xfov_deg):\n        # Glass angle and TIR pitch (in mm)\n        tg_rad = np.radians(xfov_to_glass(theta_air))\n        pitch  = 2.0 * T_WG_MM * np.tan(tg_rad)   # mm\n\n        # Zone index for each starting position\n        zone_of = np.searchsorted(bounds[1:], x0)\n        zone_of = np.clip(zone_of, 0, n_zones - 1)\n\n        # Primary coupling: η_T of the zone at x0\n        eta_c = np.array([T_zones[z][ai] for z in zone_of], dtype=float)\n\n        # Secondary interactions: multiply by η_R for each bounce inside coupler\n        # Vectorised over bounces; max bounces = ceil(L/pitch)\n        if pitch > 0:\n            max_bounces = int(np.ceil(L / pitch)) + 1\n        else:\n            max_bounces = 0\n\n        for n in range(1, max_bounces + 1):\n            xn = x0 + n * pitch\n            inside = xn < L\n            if not np.any(inside):\n                break\n            zn = np.searchsorted(bounds[1:], xn[inside])\n            zn = np.clip(zn, 0, n_zones - 1)\n            r_vals = np.array([R_zones[z][ai] for z in zn], dtype=float)\n            eta_c[inside] *= r_vals\n\n        coupling.append(float(np.mean(eta_c)))\n\n    return np.array(coupling)\n\n\ndef compute_mfe(T_zones, R_zones, xfov_deg=XFOV_DEG):\n    \"\"\"Return (mfe, coupling_array).\"\"\"\n    coupling = compute_coupling_efficiency(T_zones, R_zones, xfov_deg)\n    mfe = float(np.min(coupling))\n    return mfe, coupling\n\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Plotting\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Default starting geometry (from Table 1, with estimated x_p / period_y)\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n#\n# Geometry interpretation (confirmed by SEM in Fig 5 and paper text):\n#   • The grating period is in x (Λ_x = 453 nm).\n#   • Each unit cell has a nano-beam (running the full y-extent) and a\n#     nano-pillar (circular cylinder).\n#   • For zones 2 & 3 the pillar diameter d_p EXCEEDS Λ_y, so the pillar is\n# _lambda_y_from_truncation(r_nm: float, w_p_nm: float) -> float:\n    \"\"\"Λ_y given pillar radius r_p and chord width w_p at y-boundary.\"\"\"\n    val = r_nm**2 - (w_p_nm / 2.0)**2\n    return The key bottleneck is the MFE formula itself — at XFOV=-10°, TIR pitch ≈ 0.874 mm, so rays enter zone 1 (0-1.06 mm) and bounce to zone 1 again (0.874 mm in), losing most power due to η_R,1 ≈ 8.1%\n3. To break through 25%, I'd need to either boost zone 1's η_R while keeping η_T high, push zone 3's η_T from 16.4% up to ~25%, or switch to direct MFE optimization across all three zones jointly instead of tuning them independently.\n\nThe cleanest path forward would be a joint optimization that maximizes MFE directly, though that scales to 12 parameters with 21 constraints.\n\nA more practical alternative is to lock zones 1 and 2 since they're already performing well, then focus a zone-3-only optimization on hitting η_T ≥ 25% at XFOV=-10° while keeping η_R ≥ 60%. Let me reconsider the MFE formula more carefully—at XFOV=-10°, the pitch calculation simplifies to zero, which might be the key constraint I'm missing. Zone 2 spans from 1.06 to 2.07 mm, where rays either stay in zone 2 or bounce into zone 3 depending on their starting position, making up about 33.7% of the rays. Zone 3 covers the remaining 31% of rays from 2.07 to 3.0 mm, but most of these exit the coupler after their first bounce since they land beyond 3.0 mm. The total coupling efficiency now depends on the primary transmission efficiency.\n\nThe transmission efficiency determines how much power initially enters the waveguide, while reflection efficiency controls losses on subsequent bounces. At XFOV=-10°, I need high transmission across all zones and strong reflections in zones that experience secondary bounces. Looking at the current performance, zone 1 has decent transmission at 76% but terrible reflection at only 8.1%, zone 2 sits at 53% transmission with 34% reflection, and zone 3 is the real problem with just 16.4% transmission despite having good reflection at 69.1%. The critical issue is zone 3's weak transmission at the extreme angle, compounded by zone 1's poor reflection efficiency.\n\nLet me trace through the actual coupling efficiency formula—each ray entering at position x₀ in zone 1 gets multiplied by transmission once, then by reflection at each subsequent bounce location. At XFOV=-10° with the pitch of 0.874 mm, rays starting in the first part of zone 1 bounce through multiple zone 1 positions, giving me a coupling efficiency around 0.5% because I'm multiplying 76% transmission by the square of 8.1% reflection. For rays starting further along, the first bounce lands in zone 2 and then zone 3, which creates a different efficiency profile. For rays starting in zone 2, the first bounce lands in zone 2 or 3 with moderate reflection losses, but zone 3 rays escape after the primary coupling with no bounces, which significantly drags down the overall efficiency to around 16.4%. + 0.051 = 0.198 ≈ 20%. So I need to push zone 3's efficiency from 16.4% up to at least 25% to hit my target. The strategy is to optimize zone 3 directly for maximum MFE contribution at XFOV=-10° while keeping zones 1 and 2 fixed, since they're already performing reasonably well.\n\nI'm reconsidering the merit function for zone 3 — it should target η_T ≥ 25% specifically at XFOV=-10° (not averaged) while maintaining η_R ≥ 60% at that same point. Zone 1 is already good (η_T≈76%, η_R≈8%). Keep but re-scan to improve.\n2. Zones 2/3 are the bottleneck. Scan (x_p, Λ_y) grid targeting ideal targets\n   (η_T, η_R) = (0.54, 0.46) and (0.27, 0.73) FROM THE PAPER (Fig 2e).\n3. Minimize IDEAL loss at XFOV=-10° (the MFE-limiting angle) with STRONG\n   weighting, since the key issue is η_T,3(-10°)=16.4% (needs ≥25%).\n4. Run Nelder-Mead from best scan point.\n5. requires the right (x_p, Λ_y) combination.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport json, time\nimport numpy as np\nfrom scipy.optimize import minimize\n\nfrom rcwa_metasurface import (\n    XFOV_DEG, THETA_GLASS, PERIOD_X,\n    IDEAL_TARGETS, REALISTIC_TARGETS,\n    N_BK7, N_TIO2, N_AIR, HEIGHT_NM,\n    run_rcwa, angle_sweep, compute_mfe,\n    plot_efficiency_curves, plot_mfe_curve, plot_zone_geometry,\n)\n\nRESULTS_FILE = \"zone_results.json\"\n\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Bottleneck angle index: XFOV=-10° (index 0)\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nIDX_NEG10 = 0   # XFOV_DEG[0] = -10.0°\nIDX_ZERO  = 10  # XFOV_DEG[10] = 0.0°\nIDX_POS10 = 20  # XFOV_DEG[20] = +10.0°\n\n# Ideal targets from paper Fig 2(e)\n# These assume R[-1,0] ≈ 0 (sum = 100%)\nT_IDEAL = [0.96, 0.54, 0.27]\nR_IDEAL = [0.04, 0.46, 0.73]\n\n# Fallback realistic targets (R[-1,0] included)\nT_REAL = [0.75, 0.52, 0.26]\nR_REAL = [0.08, 0.35, 0.60]\n\nnG_SCAN = 51    # harmonics for scans\nnG_OPT  = 51    # harmonics for optimization\n\n\ndef merit_zone_ideal(x, zone_id, tgt_T, tgt_R, nG=nG_OPT):\n    \"\"\"\n    Merit function: weighted MSE at XFOV=-10° (weight 8) and remaining angles.\n    Targets ideal (sum=100%) to minimise R[-1,0] loss.\n    \"\"\"\n    w, r, xp, ly = x\n    if w < 30 or w > 280 or r < 15 or r > 200:\n        return 1e6\n    if xp < 20 or xp > PERIOD_X + r + 30:\n        return 1e6\n    if ly < 60 or ly > 500:\n        return 1e6\n\n    # Evaluate at three representative angles: -10°, 0°, +10°\n    angles = [THETA_GLASS[IDX_NEG10], THETA_GLASS[IDX_ZERO], THETA_GLASS[IDX_POS10]]\n    weights = [8.0, 1.0, 1.0]  # very heavy weight on bottleneck angle\n\n    loss = 0.0\n    for tg, wt in zip(angles, weights):\n        T, R = run_rcwa(w, r, xp, ly, tg, nG=nG)\n        loss += wt * ((T - tgt_T)**2 + (R - tgt_R)**2)\n\n    return loss / sum(weights)\n\n\ndef merit_zone_full(x, zone_id, tgt_T, tgt_R, nG=nG_OPT):\n    \"\"\"Merit over full XFOV, heavy weight at -10°.\"\"\"\n    w, r, xp, ly = x\n    if w < 30 or w > 280 or r < 15 or r > 200:\n        return 1e6\n    if xp < 20 or xp > PERIOD_X + r + 30:\n        return 1e6\n    if ly < 60 or ly > 500:\n        return 1e6\n\n    T_arr, R_arr = angle_sweep((w, r, xp, ly), nG=nG)\n\n    weights = np.ones(len(XFOV_DEG))\n    weights[IDX_NEG10] = 8.0   # -10° bottleneck\n    weights /= weights.sum()\n\n    T_mean = float(np.dot(weights, T_arr))\n    R_mean = float(np.dot(weights, R_arr))\n    return (T_mean - tgt_T)**2 + (R_mean - tgt_R)**2\n\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# 2-D grid scan over (x_p, Λ_y) for fixed (w, r)\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef grid_scan_xp_ly(\n    zone_id: int,\n    w_nm: float,\n    r_nm: float,\n    xp_range,   # array of x_p values\n    ly_range,   # array of Λ_y values\n    tgt_T: float,\n    tgt_R: float,\n    nG: int = nG_SCAN,\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Scan (x_p, Λ_y) at XFOV=-10° to find best starting geometry.\n    Returns sorted list of (loss, [w, r, xp, ly]).\n    \"\"\"\n    tg0 = THETA_GLASS[IDX_NEG10]\n    results = []\n\n    n_total = len(xp_range) * len(ly_range)\n    print(f\"  Grid scan zone {zone_id+1}: {len(xp_range)} × {len(ly_range)} = {n_total} points\")\n    t0 = time.time()\n\n    for i, xp in enumerate(xp_range):\n        for j, ly in enumerate(ly_range):\n            try:\n                T, R = run_rcwa(w_nm, r_nm, xp, ly, tg0, nG=nG)\n                loss = (T - tgt_T)**2 + (R - tgt_R)**2\n                results.append((loss, T, R, [w_nm, r_nm, xp, ly]))\n            except Exception:\n                pass\n        if (i + 1) % 3 == 0:\n            elapsed = time.time() - t0\n            rate = (i + 1) * len(ly_range) / elapsed\n            remaining = (n_total - (i + 1) * len(ly_range)) / rate\n            print(f\"    ... {(i+1)*len(ly_range)}/{n_total} done \"\n                  f\"({elapsed:.0f}s elapsed, ~{remaining:.0f}s left)\")\n\n    results.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])\n    print(f\"  Best: loss={results[0][0]:.5f}  \"\n          f\"η_T={results[0][1]*100:.1f}%  η_R={results[0][2]*100:.1f}%  \"\n          f\"params={results[0][3]}\")\n    return results\n\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Local optimisation from a starting point\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef local_opt(x0, zone_id, tgt_T, tgt_R, maxiter=400, nG=nG_OPT):\n    \"\"\"Nelder-Mead from x0.\"\"\"\n    def merit(x):\n        return merit_zone_full(x, zone_id, tgt_T, tgt_R, nG=nG)\n\n    res = minimize(merit, x0, method=\"Nelder-Mead\",\n                   options={\"maxiter\": maxiter, \"xatol\": 0.5, \"fatol\": 1e-5})\n    best = res.x\n    T_arr, R_arr = angle_sweep(tuple(best), nG=nG)\n    return {\n        \"params\": best.tolist(),\n        \"T_array\": T_arr.tolist(),\n        \"R_array\": R_arr.tolist(),\n        \"loss\": float(res.fun),\n        \"success\": res.success,\n    }\n\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Main optimisation pipeline\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef main():\n    print(\"\\n\" + \"#\"*65)\n    print(\"#  MFE-focused optimisation  (targets: ideal paper prev = json.load(fh)\n        for k in range(3):\n            z = prev[\"zones\"][k]\n            zone_results.append({\n                \"params\":  z[\"params\"],\n                \"T_array\": [t/100 for t in z[\"T_pct\"]],\n                \"R_array\": [r/100 for r in z[\"R_pct\"]],\n                \"source\":  z.get(\"source\", \"loaded\"),\n            })\n        print(\"Loaded previous best results from zone_results.json\")\n    except Exception:\n        from rcwa_metasurface import TABLE1_PARAMS\n        for k, p in enumerate(TABLE1_PARAMS):\n            T_arr, R_arr = angle_sweep(tuple(p), nG=nG_SCAN)\n            zone_results.append({\n                \"params\": list(p),\n                \"T_array\": T_arr.tolist(),\n                \"R_array\": R_arr.tolist(),\n                \"source\": \"Table1\",\n            })\n        print(\"Using Table 1 starting geometry.\")\n\n    # Print current MFE\n    T_z = [np.array(r[\"T_array\"]) for r in zone_results]\n in zone_results]\n\n    # ── Zone 3 focused optimisation (most critical) ───────────────────────────\n    print(\"\\n\" + \"=\"*65)\n    print(\"ZONE 3  —  Scan + optimise (ideal target: η_T=27%, best_zone3_res  = res\n\n    print(f\"\\n  Best zone 3: loss={best_zone3_loss:.5f}\")\n    T3b = np.array(best_zone3_res[\"T_array\"])[IDX_NEG10] * 100\n    R3b = η_R(-10°)={R1b:.1f}%  ({dt:.0f}s)\")\n\n    if res1[\"loss\"] < merit_zone_full(p1_cur, 0, T_IDEAL[0], R_IDEAL[0]):\n        best_results[0] = res1\n\n    # ── Compute final MFE print(f\"  Coupling range: {coupling.min()*100:.1f}% – {coupling.max()*100:.1f}%\")\n\n    params_list = [r[\"params\"] for r in best_results]\n    for k in range(3):\n        T0 = f\"sum={T0+R0:.1f}%  \"\n              f\"[w={w:.0f} r={r:.0f} xp={xp:.0f} Ly={ly:.0f}]\")\n\n    # ── Save JSON ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    out = {\n         for k in range(3)\n        ],\n    }\n    with open(RESULTS_FILE, \"w\") as fh:\n        json.dump(out, fh, indent=2)\n    print(f\"\\nSaved: {RESULTS_FILE}\")\n\n    # ── Plots Zone 1 is already good (η_T≈76%, η_R≈8%). Keep but re-scan to improve.\n2. Zones 2/3 are the bottleneck. Scan (x_p, Λ_y) grid targeting ideal targets\n   (η_T, η_R) = (0.54, 0.46) and (0.27, 0.73) FROM THE PAPER (Fig 2e).\n3. Minimize IDEAL loss at XFOV=-10° (the MFE-limiting angle) with STRONG\n   weighting, since the key issue is η_T,3(-10°)=16.4% (needs ≥25%).\n4. Run Nelder-Mead from best scan point.\n5. Compute final MFE; save JSON + PNG.\n\nThe key physics: at θ_glass=41.19° (XFOV=-10°) we need to suppress the\nbackward-diffracted R[-1,0] order in glass so that T[-1,0] + R[0,0] ≈ 100%.\nThis requires the right (x_p, Λ_y) combination.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport json, time\nimport numpy as np\nfrom scipy.optimize import minimize\n\nfrom rcwa_metasurface import (\n    XFOV_DEG, THETA_GLASS, PERIOD_X,\n    IDEAL_TARGETS, REALISTIC_TARGETS,\n    N_BK7, N_TIO2, N_AIR, HEIGHT_NM,\n    run_rcwa, angle_sweep, compute_mfe,\n    plot_efficiency_curves, plot_mfe_curve, plot_zone_geometry,\n)\n\nRESULTS_FILE = \"zone_results.json\"\n\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Bottleneck angle index: XFOV=-10° (index 0)\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nIDX_NEG10 = 0   # XFOV_DEG[0] = -10.0°\nIDX_ZERO  = 10  # XFOV_DEG[10] = 0.0°\nIDX_POS10 = 20  # XFOV_DEG[20] = +10.0°\n\n# Ideal targets from paper Fig 2(e)\n# These assume R[-1,0] ≈ 0 (sum = 100%)\nT_IDEAL = [0.96, 0.54, 0.27]\nR_IDEAL = [0.04, 0.46, 0.73]\n\n# Fallback realistic targets (R[-1,0] included)\nT_REAL = [0.75, 0.52, 0.26]\nR_REAL = [0.08, 0.35, 0.60]\n\nnG_SCAN = 51    # harmonics for scans\nnG_OPT  = 51    # harmonics for optimization\n\n\ndef merit_zone_ideal(x, zone_id, tgt_T, tgt_R, nG=nG_OPT):\n    \"\"\"\n    Merit function: weighted MSE at XFOV=-10° (weight 8) and remaining angles.\n    Targets ideal (sum=100%) to minimise R[-1,0] loss.\n    \"\"\"\n    w, r, xp, ly = x\n    if w < 30 or w > 280 or r < 15 or r > 200:\n        return 1e6\n    if xp < 20 or xp > PERIOD_X + r + 30:\n        return 1e6\n    if ly < 60 or ly > 500:\n        return 1e6\n\n    # Evaluate at three representative angles: -10°, 0°, +10°\n    angles = [THETA_GLASS[IDX_NEG10], THETA_GLASS[IDX_ZERO], THETA_GLASS[IDX_POS10]]\n    weights = [8.0, 1.0, 1.0]  # very heavy weight on bottleneck angle\n\n    loss = 0.0\n    for tg, wt in zip(angles, weights):\n        T, R = run_rcwa(w, r, xp, ly, tg, nG=nG)\n        loss += wt * ((T - tgt_T)**2 + (R - tgt_R)**2)\n\n    return loss / sum(weights)\n\n\ndef merit_zone_full(x, zone_id, tgt_T, tgt_R, nG=nG_OPT):\n    \"\"\"Merit over full XFOV, heavy weight at -10°.\"\"\"\n    w, r, xp, ly = x\n    if w < 30 or w > 280 or r < 15 or r > 200:\n        return 1e6\n    if xp < 20 or xp > PERIOD_X + r + 30:\n        return 1e6\n    if ly < 60 or ly > 500:\n        return 1e6\n\n    T_arr, R_arr = angle_sweep((w, r, xp, ly), nG=nG)\n\n    weights = np.ones(len(XFOV_DEG))\n    weights[IDX_NEG10] = 8.0   # -10° bottleneck\n    weights /= weights.sum()\n\n    T_mean = float(np.dot(weights, T_arr))\n    R_mean = float(np.dot(weights, R_arr))\n    return (T_mean - tgt_T)**2 + (R_mean - tgt_R)**2\n\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# 2-D grid scan over (x_p, Λ_y) for fixed (w, r)\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef grid_scan_xp_ly(\n    zone_id: int,\n    w_nm: float,\n    r_nm: float,\n    xp_range,   # array of x_p values\n    ly_range,   # array of Λ_y values\n    tgt_T: float,\n    tgt_R: float,\n    nG: int = nG_SCAN,\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Scan (x_p, Λ_y) at XFOV=-10° to find best starting geometry.\n    Returns sorted list of (loss, [w, r, xp, ly]).\n    \"\"\"\n    tg0 = THETA_GLASS[IDX_NEG10]\n    results = []\n\n    n_total = len(xp_range) * len(ly_range)\n    print(f\"  Grid scan zone {zone_id+1}: {len(xp_range)} × {len(ly_range)} = {n_total} points\")\n    t0 = time.time()\n\n    for i, xp in enumerate(xp_range):\n        for j, ly in enumerate(ly_range):\n            try:\n                T, R = run_rcwa(w_nm, r_nm, xp, ly, tg0, nG=nG)\n                loss = (T - tgt_T)**2 + (R - tgt_R)**2\n                results.append((loss, T, R, [w_nm, r_nm, xp, ly]))\n            except Exception:\n                pass\n        if (i + 1) % 3 == 0:\n            elapsed = time.time() - t0\n            rate = (i + 1) * len(ly_range) / elapsed\n            remaining = (n_total - (i + 1) * len(ly_range)) / rate\n            print(f\"    ... {(i+1)*len(ly_range)}/{n_total} done \"\n                  f\"({elapsed:.0f}s elapsed, ~{remaining:.0f}s left)\")\n\n    results.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])\n    print(f\"  Best: loss={results[0][0]:.5f}  \"\n          f\"η_T={results[0][1]*100:.1f}%  η_R={results[0][2]*100:.1f}%  \"\n          f\"params={results[0][3]}\")\n    return results\n\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Local optimisation from a starting point\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef local_opt(x0, zone_id, tgt_T, tgt_R, maxiter=400, nG=nG_OPT):\n    \"\"\"Nelder-Mead from x0.\"\"\"\n    def merit(x):\n        return merit_zone_full(x, zone_id, tgt_T, tgt_R, nG=nG)\n\n    res = minimize(merit, x0, method=\"Nelder-Mead\",\n                   options={\"maxiter\": maxiter, \"xatol\": 0.5, \"fatol\": 1e-5})\n    best = res.x\n    T_arr, R_arr = angle_sweep(tuple(best), nG=nG)\n    return {\n        \"params\": best.tolist(),\n        \"T_array\": T_arr.tolist(),\n        \"R_array\": R_arr.tolist(),\n        \"loss\": float(res.fun),\n        \"success\": res.success,\n    }\n\n\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Main optimisation pipeline\n# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef main():\n    print(\"\\n\" + \"#\"*65)\n    print(\"#  MFE-focused optimisation  (targets: ideal paper
